# Drill: Build a Control Chart

## Drill Purpose

Build fluency with the qcc control chart workflow: R chart → X̄ chart → identify out-of-control → revise limits → process capability.

## Trigger

Use this drill when you have subgroup measurement data and need to determine whether a process is in statistical control and whether it meets specification limits.

## Fixed Sequence

1. Load data (subgroup matrix: rows = subgroups, cols = observations)
2. Build **R chart first** — estimates σ̂
3. Build **X̄ chart** using σ̂ from R chart
4. Identify out-of-control subgroups (`$violations`)
5. Remove and **revise limits**
6. Compute **Cp and Cpk** against spec limits
7. State conclusion in teacher's phrasing

## Setup


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman::p_load(pacman, qcc)
```
:::


## Worked Example: Piston Rings


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
data("pistonrings")
diameter <- qcc.groups(pistonrings$diameter, pistonrings$sample)

r_chart <- qcc(diameter, type = "R", nsigmas = 3,
               title = "R Chart — Piston Ring Diameter")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![R Chart — check for out-of-control range](build-control-chart_files/figure-html/r-chart-1.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cat("R̄  (center):", round(r_chart$center, 6), "\n")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
R̄  (center): 0.023425 
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cat("UCL_R:", round(r_chart$limits[2], 6), "\n")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
UCL_R: 0.049531 
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cat("R chart violations:", r_chart$violations$beyond.limits, "\n")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
R chart violations:  
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
xbar_chart <- qcc(diameter, type = "xbar", nsigmas = 3,
                  std.dev = sd.R(diameter),
                  title = "X̄ Chart — Piston Ring Diameter")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![X̄ Chart — check for out-of-control means](build-control-chart_files/figure-html/xbar-chart-1.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cat("x̄̄  (center):", round(xbar_chart$center, 6), "\n")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
x̄̄  (center): 74.0036 
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cat("UCL:", round(xbar_chart$limits[2], 6), "\n")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
UCL: 74.01712 
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cat("LCL:", round(xbar_chart$limits[1], 6), "\n")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
LCL: 73.99009 
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cat("σ̂ :", round(xbar_chart$std.dev, 6), "\n")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
σ̂ : 0.010071 
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cat("X̄ chart violations:", xbar_chart$violations$beyond.limits, "\n")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
X̄ chart violations: 38 39 
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Spec limits: LSL = 73.95, USL = 74.05
pc <- process.capability(xbar_chart, spec.limits = c(73.95, 74.05))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Process capability](build-control-chart_files/figure-html/capability-1.png){width=672}
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Process Capability Analysis

Call:
process.capability(object = xbar_chart, spec.limits = c(73.95,     74.05))

Number of obs = 200          Target = 74
       Center = 74              LSL = 73.95
       StdDev = 0.01007         USL = 74.05

Capability indices:

      Value   2.5%  97.5%
Cp    1.655  1.492  1.817
Cp_l  1.774  1.623  1.926
Cp_u  1.536  1.403  1.668
Cp_k  1.536  1.378  1.693
Cpm   1.558  1.397  1.719

Exp<LSL 0%	 Obs<LSL 0%
Exp>USL 0%	 Obs>USL 0%
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
print(pc, digits = 6)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
$nobs
[1] 200

$center
[1] 74.0036

$std.dev
[1] 0.0100709

$target
[1] 74

$spec.limits
  LSL   USL 
73.95 74.05 

$indices
       Value    2.5%   97.5%
Cp   1.65493 1.49237 1.81728
Cp_l 1.77425 1.62291 1.92558
Cp_u 1.53561 1.40319 1.66802
Cp_k 1.53561 1.37783 1.69339
Cpm  1.55811 1.39702 1.71899

$exp
Exp < LSL Exp > USL 
        0         0 

$obs
Obs < LSL Obs > USL 
        0         0 
```


:::
:::


## Practice Variation: p-Chart

Run the p-chart interactively. Edit the `qcc()` call to explore different chart options.

```{webr-r}
#| label: p-chart-interactive

library(qcc)

data("orangejuice")

p_chart <- qcc(data  = orangejuice$D,
               type  = "p",
               sizes = orangejuice$size,
               title = "p-Chart — Orange Juice")

cat("\np̄:", round(p_chart$center, 4), "\n")
cat("Out-of-control:", p_chart$violations$beyond.limits, "\n")
```

## Reflection Questions

1. For the piston ring X̄ chart: is the process in statistical control? In control but not capable?
2. Is Cpk ≥ 1.33? What does that mean for the process?
3. For the p-chart: which lots should be investigated for special causes?
